# Optional: Spatial Data Analysis in R {.unnumbered}

Spatial data analysis is an important skill in forestry and
natural-resources work, but it is now treated as **optional extension
material** in this book. The required FRST 232 pathway focuses on Excel, R,
tidyverse workflows, data cleaning, joins, visualization, an **integrated
case study**, and **communication**.

If you want additional help with spatial data analysis in R, you can use the
optional spatial chapters — **Spatial Data in R with `sf`** and **Map
Forestry Data with `leaflet`** — kept as a separate resource. They cover
coordinate reference systems, reading and reprojecting spatial files,
distances and buffers (`sf`), static thematic maps with `geom_sf()`, and
interactive web maps with `leaflet`, using the open CSRD parks dataset.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Spatial data analysis in R — additional support
Continue to the optional spatial-data resource:

> **[Spatial Data Analysis in R — additional support](https://subornaa.github.io/spatial-analysis-r/)**
>
> Direct link (works in print too): **<https://subornaa.github.io/spatial-analysis-r/>**
:::
